# terrible_cisco_aci_aci_access_port_to_interface_policy_leaf_profile (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `access_port_selector` (String) The name of the Fabric access policy leaf interface profile access port selector.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The description to assign to the `access_port_selector`
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `fex_id` (Number) Id of the fex profile, a valid FEX ID is between 101 to 199.
- `fex_profile` (String) The name of the Fex Profile. Value of the fex_profile is overridden by the policy_group.
- `from_card` (String) **Deprecated** Starting with Ansible 2.8 we recommend using `cisco.aci.aci_access_port_block_to_access_port` instead. The parameter will be removed in Ansible 2.12. HORIZONTALLINE The beginning (from-range) of the card range block for the leaf access port block.
- `from_port` (String) **Deprecated** Starting with Ansible 2.8 we recommend using `cisco.aci.aci_access_port_block_to_access_port` instead. The parameter will be removed in Ansible 2.12. HORIZONTALLINE The beginning (from-range) of the port range block for the leaf access port block.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `interface_profile` (String) The name of the Fabric access policy leaf interface profile.
- `interface_type` (String) The type of interface for the static EPG deployment. The interface_type fex_profile can not be configured with a profile of type fex.
- `leaf_port_blk_description` (String) **Deprecated** Starting with Ansible 2.8 we recommend using `cisco.aci.aci_access_port_block_to_access_port` instead. The parameter will be removed in Ansible 2.12. HORIZONTALLINE The description to assign to the `leaf_port_blk`
- `policy_group` (String) The name of the fabric access policy group to be associated with the leaf interface profile interface selector.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `port_blk` (String) **Deprecated** Starting with Ansible 2.8 we recommend using `cisco.aci.aci_access_port_block_to_access_port` instead. The parameter will be removed in Ansible 2.12. HORIZONTALLINE The name of the Fabric access policy leaf interface profile access port block.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Use `present` or `absent` for adding or removing. Use `query` for listing an object or multiple objects.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `to_card` (String) **Deprecated** Starting with Ansible 2.8 we recommend using `cisco.aci.aci_access_port_block_to_access_port` instead. The parameter will be removed in Ansible 2.12. HORIZONTALLINE The end (to-range) of the card range block for the leaf access port block.
- `to_port` (String) **Deprecated** Starting with Ansible 2.8 we recommend using `cisco.aci.aci_access_port_block_to_access_port` instead. The parameter will be removed in Ansible 2.12. HORIZONTALLINE The end (to-range) of the port range block for the leaf access port block.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `type` (String) The type of access port to be created under respective profile.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `current` (String) The existing configuration from the APIC after the module has finished
- `error` (String) The error information as returned from the APIC
- `filter_string` (String) The filter string used for the request
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `method` (String) The HTTP method used for the request to the APIC
- `previous` (String) The original configuration from the APIC before the module has started
- `proposed` (String) The assembled configuration from the user-provided parameters
- `raw` (String) The raw output returned by the APIC REST API (xml or json)
- `response` (String) The HTTP response from the APIC
- `sent` (String) The actual/minimal configuration pushed to the APIC
- `status` (Number) The HTTP status from the APIC
- `url` (String) The HTTP url used for the request to the APIC
